title: Chrysanthemum poteriifolium
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum poteriifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, which has been reclassified to be part of the genus Tanacetum in some classifications. However, Chrysanthemum poteriifolium is still widely recognized under the genus Chrysanthemum. The classification of this plant can be complex, with different sources providing varying classifications. Further research may be needed to determine the most up-to-date and accurate classification for Chrysanthemum poteriifolium.
uses: Chrysanthemum poteriifolium is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various inflammatory conditions, including fever and rheumatism. intro: Chrysanthemum poteriifolium, also known as Yellow Chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as fever, headache, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum poteriifolium include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, which is also known as Jing Jue Teng, to treat a range of health conditions.
